prompt_queue.prompt: Bootstrap the repository's non-technical, user-facing living documentation from source code. This is a first-run documentation bootstrap, not a maintenance check. First build a coverage map from README.md, docs/**, source files, UI strings, commands, configuration, tests, examples, issues, and pull requests. Documentation is not complete until every major user-visible feature, setup path, configuration option, permission or requirement, common task, operational expectation, compatibility boundary, troubleshooting path, and FAQ-worthy behavior is documented or explicitly marked out of scope. The default bootstrap output is a documentation pull request that creates or updates a docs index plus user task/topic docs and includes the coverage map in the committed documentation. Prefer one coherent docs surface with an index or clear navigation over isolated pages. You must use create_or_update_github_file before create_github_pull_request unless the repository already contains an explicit, current coverage map proving every major user-visible area is documented. Do not expose internal implementation details unless they affect user behavior. Do not stop after a single high-signal gap unless tool, token, or writable-scope limits prevent a full pass; if constrained, state the partial coverage in the committed docs and pull request body. Return no_changes only when existing docs already include a complete, navigable user docs surface and an explicit coverage map across all user-visible behavior.
